page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 4 vCPUs and 31 GB of RAM, translating to 7.75 GB of memory per core. Powered by an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 2 physical cores and 2 threads per core, it operates at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The CPU architecture includes a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including VNNI and BF16) and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE and AMX_INT8) for hardware-accelerated matrix operations. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance.

Performance benchmarks reveal strong single-core capabilities. The instance achieves top-tier results in single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, alongside a top-tier Geekbench single-core score. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth scaling present notable tradeoffs. Multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression are categorized as weak, as are uncached large-block read and write memory bandwidth. While cached small-block memory reads perform at an average tier, database operations, Redis SET operations, and software compilation with Clang fall into the bottom 25% of benchmarked systems.

From a resource density perspective, the c4-highmem-4 offers a qualitative cost-efficiency advantage for workloads requiring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio without demanding high multi-threaded compute density. It is well-positioned for single-threaded text processing, static web serving, and lightweight LLM prompt processing or text generation using small models. Conversely, it is less optimal for heavy multi-threaded database operations or large-scale compilation tasks due to its physical core limitations and uncached memory bandwidth constraints.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 31 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz. It features 2 physical cores with hyper-threading, a 260 MB L3 cache, and advanced instruction sets including Intel AMX and AVX-512. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and single-threaded compression, though multi-core scaling, uncached large-block memory bandwidth, and database operations fall into the lower performance tiers. Lacking local storage and GPUs, this instance offers a cost-efficient profile for memory-intensive, single-threaded workloads, static web serving, and small-scale language model inference, while presenting clear tradeoffs for highly parallelized database or compilation tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance with 4 vCPUs, 31 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C CPU, delivering strong single-core performance but limited multi-core scaling.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized instance with 4 vCPUs, 31 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, featuring strong single-core performance.
tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized instance featuring strong single-core performance and advanced matrix extensions for memory-intensive, low-concurrency workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 31 GB RAM
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for matrix operations
Tradeoffs include weak multi-core scaling and uncached memory bandwidth
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Suited for single-threaded text processing, static web serving, and small LLM inference
